understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to win each level by placing all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if there are multiple goals, every goal must be occupied simultaneously to trigger WIN and progress through all levels until the final win.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects; some adjacent objects can be pushable and will move along if pushed. Levels can have multiple selectable pieces, and control switches using CLICK on the exact pixel/grid coordinate of another selectable piece. Walls generally prevent movement, but may have varying blocking behavior, so attempted moves are sometimes needed to learn interactions; the movement_blocked hints indicate adjacency but don't guarantee a move will fail.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear boxed in by walls; try moving right to test if any wall is actually passable/has a hidden exit.
history.reasoning: We appear boxed in by walls; try moving right to test if any wall is actually passable/has a hidden exit.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable to see if it has an opening/move behavior.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable to see if it has an opening/move behavior.

history.summary: Turn 3: CLICK | This selectable is boxed in by walls on all four sides, so switch back to the other piece which may still be able to move toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: This selectable is boxed in by walls on all four sides, so switch back to the other piece which may still be able to move toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed by walls; attempt moving right to test if the adjacent 'wall' is actually passable or a sliding boundary.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls; attempt moving right to test if the adjacent 'wall' is actually passable or a sliding boundary.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in by walls; the only possible progress is to try moving into the adjacent selectable on the right in case it can be pushed/slid.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls; the only possible progress is to try moving into the adjacent selectable on the right in case it can be pushed/slid.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | We’re fully enclosed by walls, so we must switch to the other selectable which may have access to the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re fully enclosed by walls, so we must switch to the other selectable which may have access to the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_UP | We need to occupy the goal area; moving up should slide us onto the goal tile adjacent above despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: We need to occupy the goal area; moving up should slide us onto the goal tile adjacent above despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the goal cluster; right is adjacent to goal and may allow overlap despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: Move onto the goal cluster; right is adjacent to goal and may allow overlap despite the wall hint.
